channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Firings at CBS' '60 Minutes' reflect the fight for media control in the age of Trump
item.description: The show's new leader says he fired star Scott Pelley for insubordination. Pelley says he was defending the integrity of the show's journalism after three top executives and two reporters were fired.
item.content:encoded:
The show's new leader says he fired star Scott Pelley for insubordination. Pelley says he was defending the integrity of the show's journalism after three top executives and two reporters were fired.

(Image credit: CBS Photo Archive/CBS via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Tribeca Film Festival opens, 25 years after helping New York recover from 9/11
item.description: Actor Robert De Niro and producing partner Jane Rosenthal created the festival in the wake of the terrorist attacks on the World Trade Center.
item.content:encoded:
Actor Robert De Niro and producing partner Jane Rosenthal created the festival in the wake of the terrorist attacks on the World Trade Center.

(Image credit: Michael Ochs Archives/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Sinister forces invade an upscale retirement community in 'The Boroughs'
item.description: The team behind Stranger Things has another series about a group of quirky misfits and mysterious creatures. The Boroughs is well worth seeing — as much for its veteran cast as for its story.
item.content:encoded: The team behind Stranger Things has another series about a group of quirky misfits and mysterious creatures. The Boroughs is well worth seeing — as much for its veteran cast as for its story.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: A nameless narrator attempts to understand Gertrude Stein in Deborah Levy's new novel
item.description: In 'My Year in Paris with Gertrude Stein,' the narrator, a writer, actually spends one month trying to understand Stein's genius, how she invented herself, and her relationship with Alice B. Toklas.
item.content:encoded: In 'My Year in Paris with Gertrude Stein,' the narrator, a writer, actually spends one month trying to understand Stein's genius, how she invented herself, and her relationship with Alice B. Toklas.
item.dc:creator: Heller McAlpin

item.title: 'Hellions' author Julia Elliott wins $150K fiction prize
item.description: The author is known for genre-bending stories that span Southern gothic, horror and fairy tale.
item.content:encoded:
The author is known for genre-bending stories that span Southern gothic, horror and fairy tale.

(Image credit: Forrest Clonts/Tin House)
item.dc:creator: Chloee Weiner

item.title: Mary Beard illuminates the ancient world -- and our own -- in 'Talking Classics'
item.description: As a classics professor, Beard has spent her career pondering life in the ancient world. The central question of her latest book is: What on earth was it like to be there?
item.content:encoded: As a classics professor, Beard has spent her career pondering life in the ancient world. The central question of her latest book is: What on earth was it like to be there?
item.dc:creator: Maureen Corrigan

item.title: 'Hamnet' novelist Maggie O'Farrell turns to her own family story in 'Land'
item.description: O'Farrell's new novel is based on the story of her own great, great-grandfather, and tells the story of a father and son mapping 19th-century Ireland after the devastation of the Great Famine.
item.content:encoded: O'Farrell's new novel is based on the story of her own great, great-grandfather, and tells the story of a father and son mapping 19th-century Ireland after the devastation of the Great Famine.
item.dc:creator: Sam Briger

item.title: Doctors checked Biden just after Trump debate as Jill Biden feared he had a stroke
item.description: The Biden administration previously said doctors examined the president "days" following the debate, not in the moments after. The former first lady revealed more details in her new book.
item.content:encoded:
The Biden administration previously said doctors examined the president "days" following the debate, not in the moments after. The former first lady revealed more details in her new book.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: 10 new books in June will transport you
item.description: With all that's required to reach "dream destinations" these days, another option is to walk to your local public library instead — and pick up one of these new books out in June set across time and place.
item.content:encoded: With all that's required to reach "dream destinations" these days, another option is to walk to your local public library instead — and pick up one of these new books out in June set across time and place.
item.dc:creator: Colin Dwyer

item.title: 'Why not me?': André De Shields on his Tony nomination and a life in the theatre
item.description: Broadway actor André De Shields discusses his Tony-nominated performance in CATS: The Jellicle Ball, turning 80 and his philosophy on life.
item.content:encoded:
Broadway actor André De Shields discusses his Tony-nominated performance in CATS: The Jellicle Ball, turning 80 and his philosophy on life.

(Image credit: Matthew Murphy and Evan Zimmerman )
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
